question: Was COVID-19 caused by zoonotic spillover or a research-related incident?

policies.label: Strict public evidence
policies.description: Retains public empirical and mechanistic evidence. It excludes intelligence judgments, institutional conduct, general biosafety context, and circumstantial coincidence as independent origin evidence.

policies.label: Contextual evidence
policies.description: Adds documentary records, biosafety practice, and published intelligence assessments while keeping purely circumstantial coincidence outside the assessment.

policies.label: Full available baseline
policies.description: Retains all 40 evidence notes in the supplied Carlo/Claude corpus. This is the full available baseline, not a claim to contain all evidence that exists.

assessment_method.aggregation: Within each independence cluster, retain only the maximum upstream support strength for each direction; then sum cluster contributions.
assessment_method.interpretation: The resulting balance is an exploratory sensitivity measure based on the baseline's support labels, not a calibrated probability or an independent truth judgment.

evidence_clusters.label: Market and early cases
evidence_clusters.description: Early-case geography, market linkage, ascertainment, and the composite market-origin argument.

evidence_clusters.label: Natural relatives and mechanisms
evidence_clusters.description: Natural sarbecovirus diversity, recombination, receptor binding, and distance from known relatives.

evidence_clusters.label: Furin-site features
evidence_clusters.description: Sequence-feature arguments about the furin-cleavage site and codon pair.

evidence_clusters.label: Opacity and data access
evidence_clusters.description: Withholding, deletion, disclosure disputes, publication process, and institutional reversals.

evidence_clusters.label: DEFUSE proposal
evidence_clusters.description: Three records describing the same 2018 proposal and its relationship to later viral features.

evidence_clusters.label: Market animals and samples
evidence_clusters.description: Live susceptible mammals, environmental samples, wildlife DNA, and the failure to identify an infected animal.

evidence_clusters.label: Intelligence and official assessments
evidence_clusters.description: Public summaries of intelligence judgments, congressional synthesis, and reported staff illness.

evidence_clusters.label: Laboratory capability and biosafety
evidence_clusters.description: Relevant experimental capability, containment practice, staffing concerns, and serial-passage possibility.

evidence_clusters.label: Mojiang and RaTG13 provenance
evidence_clusters.description: Mine illness, sample history, naming, and delayed disclosure around RaTG13.

evidence_clusters.label: Lineages and timing
evidence_clusters.description: Molecular-clock dating and duplicated arguments for multiple early introductions.

evidence_clusters.label: Missing progenitor or host
evidence_clusters.description: The unresolved absence of a close viral progenitor or identified intermediate host.

evidence_clusters.label: Zoonotic precedents
evidence_clusters.description: SARS-CoV-1 and MERS as reference classes for coronavirus emergence.

evidence_clusters.label: Wuhan geographic coincidence
evidence_clusters.description: The geographic co-location of outbreak recognition and a leading bat-coronavirus laboratory.

known_but_unavailable.label: Independent early-case line list
known_but_unavailable.note: The baseline repeatedly identifies raw early-case locations, exposure histories, and case-finding procedures as unavailable for independent audit.

known_but_unavailable.label: Underlying intelligence
known_but_unavailable.note: Public agency conclusions do not expose the classified sources, dependency structure, or analytic tradecraft behind them.

known_but_unavailable.label: Laboratory inventories and incident records
known_but_unavailable.note: Complete WIV sequence inventories, laboratory notebooks, incident logs, and staff medical records are not available in the supplied corpus.

known_but_unavailable.label: Contemporaneous animal and supply-chain samples
known_but_unavailable.note: The relevant live animals were removed before systematic sampling, and upstream suppliers were not comprehensively investigated in the available record.

capability_limited.label: Sequence-level genomic adjudication
capability_limited.note: The annotation pass did not independently reproduce phylogenetic, molecular-clock, codon, or recombination analyses.

capability_limited.label: Spatial and ascertainment modeling
capability_limited.note: The annotation pass did not reproduce competing spatial models from raw case data.

capability_limited.label: Non-English full-text interpretation
capability_limited.note: The imported baseline does not provide a measured language-coverage or language-reliability profile.

not_established.label: Systematic non-English source coverage
not_established.note: This prototype cannot yet prove which relevant non-English sources were excluded; it records the missing coverage measurement rather than inventing a source list.

not_established.label: Complete source universe
not_established.note: The 173 normalized citations are the sources in the supplied baseline, not an enumeration of all relevant evidence that exists.
